query: Draft a suit for partition and separate possession of ancestral joint family property situated at Bangalore. The Plaintiff and Defendants are Hindu co-owners who inherited the property from their deceased father. The property includes a residential house at No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore measuring 2400 sq.ft. and agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural measuring 2 acres. The Defendants are denying the Plaintiff's rightful 1/3rd share and refusing to partition. Claim mesne profits for exclusion from possession. Include genealogy table, schedule of properties, and prayer for appointment of Commissioner. Draft suitable for filing before the City Civil Court, Bangalore.

v4.draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT, BANGALORE

Partition suit for partition and separate possession of ancestral Hindu joint family property with appointment of Commissioner; mesne profits for exclusion from possession

Plaintiff: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, Hindu, Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}. Defendants: {{DEF1_NAME}} and {{DEF2_NAME}}, both Hindus, aged about {{DEF1_AGE}} and {{DEF2_AGE}} years respectively, Occupations: {{DEF1_OCCUPATION}} and {{DEF2_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{DEF1_ADDRESS}} and {{DEF2_ADDRESS}}. They are co-owners and heirs of the deceased father {{FATHER_NAME}}.

JURISDICTION

This Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to entertain and try this partition suit under Order XX Rule 18 CPC as the suit concerns the ancestral joint Hindu family property situate in Bangalore, comprising residential house No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore (2400 sq ft) and agricultural land Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural (2 acres). The parties are residents within the jurisdiction of Bengaluru City and Bengaluru Rural taluk, and the cause of action arises within the local limits of this Court. The pecuniary limits for partition suits are satisfied by the value of the coparcenary property claimed in this plaint.

FACTS OF THE CASE

1. The Plaintiff and Defendants are sons of late {{FATHER_NAME}} who imparted jointly to own the ancestral property; the ownership is coparcenary in nature as member of a Hindu undivided family.

2. The ancestral property comprises two assets: (i) a residential house at No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bengaluru, measuring 2400 sq ft; (ii) agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bengaluru Rural, measuring 2 acres.

3. On the death of their father, the Plaintiff and Defendants inherited the property as co-owners; the Plaintiff asserts a 1/3rd share in the joint estate.

4. The Defendants deny the Plaintiff's 1/3rd share and refuse to partition or subdivide the joint estate.

5. There has been no partition by metes and bounds or partition by sale and separate possession; the estate continues to be held jointly without demarcation.

6. The Plaintiff has been in possession of his share along with other coparceners, but the Defendants have excluded him from exclusive possession of his 1/3rd share and have prevented any effective partition.

7. The Plaintiff has repeatedly requested partition and demarcation; however, the Defendants have steadfastly refused.

8. The Plaintiff seeks an order for partition by metes and bounds and separate possession of his 1/3rd share, with the remaining co-owners continuing in possession of their shares subject to final partition.

9. The Plaintiff also claims mesne profits for the period during which he was deprived of his share and excluded from possession, till partition is effected.

10. A genealogy table illustrating the family tree and the coparcenary status is attached as Annexure A.

11. The property schedule is attached as Annexure B detailing the two properties and their respective extents.

12. Annexure C contains copies of title deeds/record evidencing co-ownership; Annexure D contains father’s death certificate; Annexure E contains any prior partition notices or correspondence with the Defendants.

LEGAL BASIS

13. Order XX Rule 18 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 empowers a Court in a suit for partition and separate possession to determine the shares and, if necessary, to direct partition by metes and bounds and to appoint a Commissioner to effect such partition.

14. Hindu Succession Act, 1956: Sections 6, 8 and 10 govern coparcenary rights and devolution of joint family property; on death of a coparcener, the property devolves by survivorship among the coparceners and female heirs as applicable, preserving the coparcenary rights in the partition context.

15. Partition Act, 1893: Sections 2, 3, 4 provide for partition of estates and the manner of partition and the role of the Court in effectuating such partition.

16. Indian Succession Act, 1925: Sections 211 and 218 relate to the mode of devolution of property on death and the rights of heirs; these provisions underpin hereditary rights in joint family property and support the Plaintiff's entitlement to a share in the partition.

17. The pleadings do not seek any sale of property at this stage; the primary relief is partition by metes and bounds with separate possession of the Plaintiff’s 1/3 share, while reserving the remaining shares for final judicial allotment.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. Origin: The cause of action first arose on the death of {{FATHER_NAME}} when the ancestral joint family property came into the succession of the Plaintiff and Defendants as coparceners and co-owne {{AMOUNT}}. Further accrual: Since the date of death of the father, the Defendants have refused to partition or demarcate the estate and have deprived the Plaintiff of exclusive possession of his rightful 1/3 share.

19. Continuing: The right to partition remains unresolved, and the Plaintiff continues to suffer ongoing exclusion from possession of his 1/3 share, justifying the present suit for partition and separate possession along with mesne profits for the period of exclusion.

LIMITATION

No limitation applies to partition between co-owners; however, if ouster or exclusion is alleged, the claim may be subject to accrual from the date exclusion became known to the Plaintiff. This suit is filed within a timely frame for partition and related relief.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

The plaint is valued for court fee and jurisdiction purposes as a partition suit; ad valorem court fee is payable under the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, calculated on the value of the Plaintiff's share (1/3rd of the ancestral property) with mesne profits claimed as a dependent head of claim. The exact amount will be determined at the time of filing and payable in accordance with law. Annexure C–E references and Schedule of properties will assist in determining the valuation.

PRAYER

20. In the premises, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to: (a) Partition the ancestral joint Hindu family property by metes and bounds and grant separate possession of the Plaintiff's 1/3 share along with allotment of demarcated boundaries;
(b) Appoint a Commissioner to divide the joint property by metes and bounds and file a report within a specified time;
(c) Award mesne profits for the period of exclusion from possession by the Defendants, to be calculated by the Commissioner;
(d) Costs of the suit;
(e) Such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Sr. No. | Description | Annexure

21. Genealogy table | Annexure A

22. Schedule of properties (No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar and Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk) | Annexure B

23. Title deeds/Record of Rights evidencing coparcenery | Annexure C

24. Death certificate of father | Annexure D

25. Any prior partition notices/communications | Annexure E

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby affirm and verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 22 of this plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge and that the documents annexed hereto and marked as Annexures A to E are genuine copies thereof. Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____. 

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF
Through: {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate

v5.draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT, BANGALORE

AT BANGALORE

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR PARTITION AND SEPARATE POSSESSION OF ANCESTRAL JOINT FAMILY PROPERTY WITH MESNE PROFITS AND APPOINTMENT OF COMMISSIONER

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

1. JURISDICTION AND VENUE
This Hon'ble Court has territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction to try and decide the suit, inasmuch as the properties mentioned in Schedule Annexure A are situate within the local limits of the City Civil Court, Bangalore, the cause of action has arisen within such jurisdiction, and the suit is within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Court as provided by law for partition suits under Order XX Rule 18 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

2. PARTIES
The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, is a Hindu individual and a co-owner of the ancestral joint family properties described in Annexure A, residing at the address stated above. The Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, is also a Hindu individual and co-owner of the same ancestral properties, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}. The Plaintiff claims a 1/3rd share in the joint family properties and seeks partition and separate possession in respect of the same; the Defendant denies the Plaintiff’s 1/3rd share and resists partition.

3. GENEALOGY TABLE
The genealogy of the family ownership of the ancestral property is attached herewith as Annexure B (Genealogy Table), which shows the father and the coparceners, including the Plaintiff and the Defendant(s). The Plaintiff states that the suit property is ancestral joint family property inherited from their deceased father, namely {{FATHER_NAME}}, as per Annexure B. (Annexure B is to be read with this plaint.)

4. SCHEDULE OF PROPERTIES
The properties which are the subject-matter of this suit and which are situate within the State of Karnataka are described in Annexure A (Schedule of Properties) and include:
(a) Residential House No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore, measuring 2400 sq.ft.; and
(b) Agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural, measuring 2 acres.
The Plaintiff claims a 1/3rd share in the aforesaid properties as coparcener of the Hindu Mitakshara joint family, the details of which are contained in Annexure A.

5. FACTS OF THE CASE
5.1 The Plaintiff and the Defendant(s) are Hindu coparceners and co-owners of the ancestral joint family properties described in Annexure A, having inherited the properties from their deceased father, {{FATHER_NAME}} (hereinafter “the father”). The Plaintiff contends that the father held the properties as joint family assets, acquired by ancestral acquisition, and that the Plaintiff’s share in the joint ancestral property is 1/3rd, as per statutory law.
5.2 The Defendant(s) have denied the Plaintiff’s rightful 1/3rd share and refused to partition the properties, thereby depriving the Plaintiff of his lawful share and his right to seek partition and separate possession.
5.3 Since the aforesaid denial and refusal, the Plaintiff has been excluded from possession of the suit properties pursuant to which the Plaintiff seeks appropriate reliefs, including partition and delivery of possession of his 1/3rd share, along with mesne profits for the period of exclusion.
5.4 The Plaintiff is amenable to partition by metes and bounds and has no objection to an orderly, court-supervised partition with accounts as may be directed by this Hon’ble Court; the Plaintiff seeks appointment of a Commissioner for local inquiry and partition, as provided under the Partition Act and the CPC.

6. CAUSE OF ACTION AND CONTINUING NATURE
6.1 This is a suit for partition and separate possession of ancestral joint family properties. The cause of action first accrued on account of the defendants’ denial of the Plaintiff’s 1/3rd share and refusal to partition, which denial constitutes obstruction to the Plaintiff’s rights as a co-owner. The cause of action is a continuing one, as the Plaintiff’s right to partition subsists and continues to accrue from day to day so long as partition and possession are not provided, and the Plaintiff remains excluded from possession. The continuing accrual continues within the jurisdiction of this Court.

7. OUSTR AND MESNE PROFITS
7.1 The Plaintiff seeks mesne profits for the exclusion from possession from the date of exclusion up to the date of decree, and thereafter as may be directed by this Court, as per the law and Court’s directions. The quantum of mesne profits will be quantified and claimed in the final decree, or as the Court deems fit, based on the accounts and evidence.

8. LIMITATION
8.1 There is no applicable limitation prescribed for partition suits between co-owners; however, if ouster or exclusion is pleaded, the date of exclusion may govern accrual of mesne profits. This Plaintiff pleads exclusion starting from the date asserted in the plaint and seeks relief within the period available under law.

9. LEGAL BASIS
9.1 ORDER XX RULE 18 CPC: Partition of immovable property and separate possession of a share in accordance with law may be ordered by metes and bounds by final decree, upon court direction and after due inquiry or as may be appropriate in the circumstances of the case.
9.2 ALTERNATIVE PLEA WITHOUT PREJUDICE: Hindu Succession Act, 1956, Sec. 6, Sec. 8, Sec. 10; Partition Act, 1893, Sec. 2, Sec. 3, Sec. 4; Indian Succession Act, 1925, Sec. 211, Sec. 218, to the extent they recognize coparcenary rights and succession among Class I heirs and coparceners in relation to the ancestral property and partition claims (where applicable to the facts of this case).
9.3 The Plaintiff seeks partition and separate possession by metes and bounds of the joint family property and a declaration of the Plaintiff’s 1/3rd share in accordance with law.
9.4 The Plaintiff also seeks appointment of a Commissioner to make a partition in terms of Section 13 of the Partition Act or equivalent court-ordered partition under Order XX Rule 18 CPC.

10. IN THE ALTERNATIVE
10.1 Without prejudice to the above, the Plaintiff pleads that if the Court is of the view that a partition by metes and bounds cannot be effected by the Court's direct decree for any reason, the Court may pass a preliminary decree for partition and appoint a Commissioner to localize the partition and report to the Court in terms of the Partition Act.

11. CO-OWNERSHIP AND DEFENCE POINTS
11.1 The Plaintiff is a co-owner and seeks partition; there is no prior partition; the Plaintiff seeks a division and separate possession in accordance with law.

12. MESNE PROFITS (DEMAND)
12.1 The Plaintiff claims mesne profits for the period of exclusion from possession and continues to claim such mesne profits as may be determined by the Court.

13. COURT FEES
13.1 The suit is a suit for partition and mesne profits; the Court fees payable shall be as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, Schedule B, based on the value of the share or relief sought, as may be assessed by the Court. (The exact amount shall be calculated and paid as per the court fee schedule at the time of filing, and a receipt will be filed with the plaint.)

14. PRAYER
In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:
(a) pleased to declare that the Plaintiff is entitled to 1/3rd share in the joint family properties described in Annexure A and to pass a final decree by metes and bounds partitioning the said properties in accordance with law and delivering possession of the Plaintiff’s share of the properties;
(b) appoint a suitable Commissioner to make partition by metes and bounds of the properties described in Annexure A and to file a partition report with the Court;
(c) grant a final decree of partition and separate possession of the Plaintiff’s share in the properties described in Annexure A, with directions for execution and delivery of possession;
(d) award mesne profits for exclusion from possession from the date of exclusion up to decree and further interest as the Court may deem fit;
(e) award costs of the suit including Court fees and advocates’ fees;
(f) pass such other and further orders as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

AND FOR THIS ACT OF KINDNESS, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL, AS IN DUTY BOUND, EVER PRAY.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS
Annexure A: Schedule of Properties (Residential House No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore; Agricultural land Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural)
Annexure B: Genealogy Table (Family tree showing father and coparceners, including Plaintiff and Defendant)
Annexure C: Title Deeds and relevant documents in support of ownership and possession

VERIFICATION
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify and state on oath that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 14 of this plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge, based on the records and documents available with me, that the contents relating to legal submissions are based upon legal advice received and are believed to be correct, that no part of the cause of action has arisen in any court other than this Hon'ble Court, and that nothing material has been concealed therefrom. Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

                                                        {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

                                                        PLAINTIFF

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}

Counsel for Plaintiff

--- END OF PLEADING ---
